---
title: "Proof-Carrying Glyphs"
---

Proof-carrying glyphs are treated here as proof interfaces. A glyph may point to a theorem id and Lean declaration name, but the theorem manifest and compiled Lean declaration remain the proof source of truth.

::: {.learning-unit}

## What To Learn Here

A glyph is useful only if it is connected to source truth. The reader should treat it as a visual handle for a theorem, dictionary terms, and metadata, not as an independent proof.

:::

<div class="warning-box">
Diagrams and glyphs are not proofs. The current proved layer covers certificate projections and finite metadata validity only.
</div>

## What To Notice

- A proof-carrying glyph records theorem and dictionary identifiers.
- Certificate projection facts make metadata inspectable.
- The Lean declaration and theorem manifest remain the proof authority.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

If a glyph shows a theorem-like diagram but its theorem id is missing from the manifest, how should the Living Book treat it?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0003"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0005"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS.md">Proof-Carrying Glyphs</a></p>

## Glyph Fixture

This fixture is generated from `manifests/glyphs/proof_glyph_fixtures.yaml` and resolved against theorem and dictionary data during site export.

<div class="glyph-index"></div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
